questions.question: CRC errors on an interface most often indicate…
questions.explanation: CRC failures mean the frame checksum failed — physical or duplex problems.

correct.explanation: CRC failures mean the frame checksum failed — physical or duplex problems.

incorrect.explanation: **Correct routing** describes a different mechanism than this question tests. The right idea: CRC failures mean the frame checksum failed — physical or duplex problems.

incorrect.explanation: **DNS failure** describes a different mechanism than this question tests. The right idea: CRC failures mean the frame checksum failed — physical or duplex problems.
incorrect.misconceptionTested: Dragging DNS into a question about a different service or layer

incorrect.explanation: **Valid VLAN tagging** describes a different mechanism than this question tests. The right idea: CRC failures mean the frame checksum failed — physical or duplex problems.

questions.question: Switch port forced full-duplex; PC auto-negotiates half-duplex. Expected symptom?
choices: Slow performance with CRC and late collisions

questions.question: True or False: An interface can show up/up while still accumulating CRC errors.
questions.explanation: True — link status does not guarantee error-free transmission.

questions.question: CRC errors appear only on a cable running parallel to power lines. Likely cause?
